title: Unit 28: Idioms and Ancient Remnants - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 28 represents the absolute pinnacle of our Grammar Podcast series: Idioms and Ancient Remnants. To reach true, effortless fluency at the B2 and C1 levels, one must master the 'ghosts' of the past. Athina, what are some high-frequency ancient dative fragments?
Εν τω μεταξύ, Δόξα τω Θεώ, Τοις μετρητοίς. Αυτά είναι παγωμένα, δεν αλλάζουν.

Correct. In the meantime, thank God, and in cash. These ancient dative constructions are syntactic constants. Additionally, formal prepositions used in business and journalism stubbornly demand the Genitive case. Athina, provide examples.
Λόγω βροχής, μέσω Αθηνών, εξαιτίας του νόμου. Οπωσδήποτε γενική.
Precisely. Furthermore, we must embrace colorful street idioms, which can be highly confusing if translated literally. Athina, what is the 'Rain' idiom?
Βρέχει καρεκλοπόδαρα. Σιγά τα λάχανα. Έγινε της κακομοίρας. Αυτά είναι για την καθημερινότητα.

Exactly. Lastly, we must observe how Greek constructs massive compound words utilizing the linking vowel 'o'. From 'fish taverns' to 'bookstores', this morphological power is essential. This concludes our academic curriculum for Unit 28. Congratulations on your linguistic journey through Modern Greek grammar.
